text:
Once upon a time in the quiet village of Everwood, there was an old bookstore that had survived countless storms. Its windows were barely visible from the street, hidden behind a thick screen of bookshelves. Among the towering piles of worn-out novels and forgotten dictionaries lived a peculiar old owl named Old Wisdom.

Every morning before dawn, Old Wisdom would fly out from his perch in the rafters and scan the rows of forgotten texts. He couldn't read them, but he knew the tales they told—the wisdom behind ancient pottery shards and sun-baked leaflets.

One crisp autumn morning, as he surveyed the assortment of forgotten lore, Old Wisdom heard a peculiar noise. It wasn't the usual rustling of pages or a tiny mouse nibbling on a dust mote. Instead, it was something much more curious—it sounded like laughter. Curious, Old Wisdom perched on a branch and glided down to investigate.

What he found was magical. A group of children, aged six to eight,
